Defines constants and functions for erasing optimal flash memory regions.

# Purpose
This C header file defines constants and function prototypes related to flash memory management. It specifies the size and address masks for sectors and subsectors using `#define` directives, which are `SECTOR_SIZE_BYTES`, `SECTOR_ADDR_MASK`, `SUBSECTOR_SIZE_BYTES`, and `SUBSECTOR_ADDR_MASK`. These constants help in aligning addresses to sector and subsector boundaries. The file also defines the start and end addresses for a shared flash region, `FLASH_REGION_SHARED_PRF_STORAGE_BEGIN` and `FLASH_REGION_SHARED_PRF_STORAGE_END`. Additionally, it declares two functions, [`flash_region_erase_optimal_range`](<#flash_region_erase_optimal_range>) and [`flash_region_erase_optimal_range_no_watchdog`](<#flash_region_erase_optimal_range_no_watchdog>), which are intended to erase specified ranges of flash memory, with the latter function variant presumably not interacting with a watchdog timer.
